No Poverty.reason: The course's focus on financial problem-solving and modeling can indirectly contribute to developing financial services, which aligns with Target 1.4's goal of increasing access to financial services for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to food security, nutrition, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare access.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education providing practical professional skills in finance, directly aligning with Targets 4.3 (tertiary education) and 4.4 (skills for employment and entrepreneurship).
evidence: develop value-added skills in spreadsheet modeling of financial applications
acquire practical professional skills - problem solving

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops professional financial skills (modeling, problem-solving) that support economic productivity (8.2), enterprise growth (8.3), and strengthen financial institutions (8.10), fostering decent work.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course teaches 'Capital budgeting' for infrastructure investment (9.1), facilitates enterprise access to finance (9.3), and uses innovative technology like 'Web crawler' (9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Financial analysis skills (ratio analysis, statistical analysis) are foundational for monitoring financial markets, which can indirectly support improved regulation under Target 10.5.

Climate Action.reason: The course teaches 'Scenario analysis' and 'Risk and return' and mentions 'sustainability issues,' which can be inferred to apply to climate-related financial risk analysis (Target 13.1).

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land management.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The core competency on 'business ethics' and financial analysis skills are relevant for identifying and preventing financial misconduct, contributing to reducing illicit flows and corruption (Targets 16.4, 16.5).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course teaches 'Web crawler' for data collection and 'statistical analysis,' directly supporting data availability for monitoring (Target 17.18) and resource mobilization (Target 17.3).
